heading: Taxpayer Assistance Orders
label: 7811 Taxpayer Assistance Orders

children.label: A
children.text: the National Taxpayer Advocate determines the taxpayer is suffering or about to suffer a significant hardship as a result of the manner in which the internal revenue laws are being administered by the Secretary; or

children.label: B
children.text: the taxpayer meets such other requirements as are set forth in regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: an immediate threat of adverse action;

children.label: B
children.text: a delay of more than 30 days in resolving taxpayer account problems;

children.label: C
children.text: the incurring by the taxpayer of significant costs (including fees for professional representation) if relief is not granted; or

children.label: D
children.text: irreparable injury to, or a long-term adverse impact on, the taxpayer if relief is not granted.

children.heading: Standard where administrative guidance not followed
children.label: 3 Standard where administrative guidance not followed
children.text: In cases where any Internal Revenue Service employee is not following applicable published administrative guidance (including the Internal Revenue Manual), the National Taxpayer Advocate shall construe the factors taken into account in determining whether to issue a Taxpayer Assistance Order in the manner most favorable to the taxpayer.

children.heading: Terms of a Taxpayer Assistance Order
children.label: b Terms of a Taxpayer Assistance Order

children.text: to release property of the taxpayer levied upon, or

children.label: B
children.text: subchapter B of chapter 70 (relating to bankruptcy and receiverships),

children.label: C
children.text: chapter 78 (relating to discovery of liability and enforcement of title), or

children.label: D
children.text: any other provision of law which is specifically described by the National Taxpayer Advocate in such order.

children.heading: Authority to modify or rescind
children.label: c Authority to modify or rescind

children.text: only by the National Taxpayer Advocate, the Commissioner of Internal Revenue, or the Deputy Commissioner of Internal Revenue, and
only if a written explanation of the reasons for the modification or rescission is provided to the National Taxpayer Advocate.

children.heading: Suspension of running of period of limitation
children.label: d Suspension of running of period of limitation

children.text: the period beginning on the date of the taxpayer’s application under subsection (a) and ending on the date of the National Taxpayer Advocate’s decision with respect to such application, and
any period specified by the National Taxpayer Advocate in a Taxpayer Assistance Order issued pursuant to such application.

children.heading: Independent action of National Taxpayer Advocate
children.label: e Independent action of National Taxpayer Advocate
children.text: Nothing in this section shall prevent the National Taxpayer Advocate from taking any action in the absence of an application under subsection (a).

children.heading: National Taxpayer Advocate
children.label: f National Taxpayer Advocate
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “National Taxpayer Advocate” includes any designee of the National Taxpayer Advocate.

children.heading: Application to persons performing services under a qualified tax collection contract
children.label: g Application to persons performing services under a qualified tax collection contract
children.text: Any order issued or action taken by the National Taxpayer Advocate pursuant to this section shall apply to persons performing services under a qualified tax collection contract (as defined in section 6306(b)) to the same extent and in the same manner as such order or action applies to the Secretary.
